claims.claim: On synthetic benchmarks with 1,000 cases and a trash ratio of 120:1, the compactor preserves 100% of marked facts at retention budgets as low as 3%, where naive tail retention preserves only 16.4%.

evidence_refs.quote: Large-language-model agents operating over long contexts routinely ingest transcripts containing high volumes of structurally obvious waste—duplicate logs, stack traces, telemetry pings, and boilerplate—alongside a small number of answer-bearing facts. We evaluate a cheap, deterministic "trash compactor" that scores lines by fact-likeness, penalizes trash and duplicates, and applies a weak recency prior, all without query awareness.
